root_cause: Intermittent packet loss or DNS path instability is causing alternating DNS failures and recoveries
evidence: Timeline shows 2 DNS failure -> recovery episode(s) for container 'api' within 20 minutes
DNS failures repeatedly target 'payments-db.default.svc.cluster.local' and are followed by recovery signals on the same container
Container 'api' is currently ready=True, indicating the issue is intermittent rather than constantly failing
Dependency service 'payments-db' still has ready endpoints during the incident window, which makes steady backend outage less likely

causes.message: The workload depends on repeated DNS lookups to reach a required upstream dependency
Timeout-style DNS failures alternate with recovery, which is more consistent with intermittent packet loss or DNS path instability than steady misconfiguration
The same container repeatedly flips between DNS failure and recovery within a short incident window
Application connectivity is unstable because dependency name resolution intermittently fails and then recovers

pod:payments-api-7c9d58b7d9-jm2sq: The pod alternates between DNS-related connectivity failure and recovery
container:api: DNS lookup failed for payments-db.default.svc.cluster.local: lookup payments-db.default.svc.cluster.local on 10.96.0.10:53: read udp 10.244.1.18:49817->10.96.0.10:53: i/o timeout
service:payments-db: Service backends remained ready while DNS failures intermittently appeared

likely_causes: The pod-to-DNS network path is intermittently dropping or delaying packets
Overlay or node networking is causing transient DNS timeouts under load
CoreDNS traffic is occasionally timing out even though the dependency service itself remains healthy
suggested_checks: Run repeated DNS lookups from a debug pod on the same node and watch for timeout spikes
Inspect CoreDNS latency, timeout, and SERVFAIL metrics during the incident window
Check node and CNI telemetry for packet drops, retransmits, or overlay-network instability
